mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month, the Fed is in active tightening mode. Equity valuations compress as real discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, FEDFUNDS=0.65% and CPIAUCSL=244.158, indicating early tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over three consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. Equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, PAYEMS=145554.0 thousand, near recent highs
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, with stronger effects during expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, RSAFS=472142.0 million, indicating sustained consumer activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, supply-side constraints are easing and labor market softening signals stagflation risk. Equity returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, INDPRO=104.5548 and UNRATE=4.8%, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or joint effect of INDPRO and UNRATE on forward returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1200 thousand units annualized, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand destruction. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, HOUST=1226.0 thousand, just above trigger level
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window during rate-hiking regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, it signals an abrupt labor market shock. Equity investors interpret this as recession signal, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, UNRATE=4.8%, stable from prior month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment shocks, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) turn positive and exceed 1.5%, indicating restrictive monetary policy. This regime-conditional signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days only when real rates are rising, not when they are stable at high levels.
candidate_trigger: Real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI inflation) > 1.5% AND rising
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, nominal FEDFUNDS=0.65% and CPIAUCSL=244.158, implying negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with real rate normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL on forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows consistently above 1.0% month-over-month, indicating robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, INDPRO=104.5548, indicating moderate supply-side activity
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).
